lesson.title: Vectors in Rⁿ
lesson.body: A vector in Rⁿ is an ordered n-tuple of real numbers representing a point or direction in n-dimensional space. Vectors support two core operations: addition (component-wise) and scalar multiplication. The dot product u·v = Σuᵢvᵢ gives a scalar measuring projection and is related to the angle between vectors by u·v = |u||v|cosθ.

rooms.question: Which of the following correctly defines the sum of vectors u = (1, 3, -2) and v = (4, -1, 5)?
If v = (2, -4, 6) and c = -3, what is the scalar multiple cv?
rooms.explanation: Scalar multiplication distributes over each component: -3·(2,-4,6) = (-6, 12, -18).

rooms.question: Compute the dot product of u = (3, -1, 2) and v = (1, 4, -2).
Two vectors u and v satisfy u·v = 0. What is the geometric interpretation?
answers: u and v have the same magnitude
rooms.explanation: Since u·v = |u||v|cosθ = 0, we have cosθ = 0, so θ = 90°. The vectors are orthogonal.

rooms.question: Order the steps to find the angle θ between u = (1, 0) and v = (1, 1):
items: Compute the dot product u\cdot v = 1
rooms.explanation: First compute the dot product (=1), then both magnitudes, then apply the arccosine formula to get 45°.

boss.name: The Null Vector
boss.taunt: I am the void between all directions. Your vectors point nowhere against me!

questions.question: Find the magnitude of the vector w = (2, -3, 6).
Which expression gives the unit vector in the direction of v = (3, 4)?
For which value of k are u = (2, k) and v = (3, -6) orthogonal?
